LR_DECAY_EPOCHS = [60, 80]","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"        channels = BASE_CHANNELS","new":"        channels = BASE_CHANNELS * CHANNEL_MULT","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"        self.blocks = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Conv2d(channels, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
        )","new":"        block_layers: list[nn.Module] = [
            nn.Conv2d(channels, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
        ]
        block_layers.extend(
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM)
            for _ in range(DEPTH)
        )
        self.blocks = nn.Sequential(*block_layers)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_function","function":"build_scheduler","old":"","new":"","anchor":"","text":"","source":"def build_scheduler(optimizer: optim.Optimizer, steps_per_epoch: int):
    if not USE_LR_SCHEDULE:
        return None
    total_steps = TRAIN_MAX_STEPS if TRAIN_MAX_STEPS is not None else steps_per_epoch * 100
    return optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(
        optimizer,
        T_max=max(1, total_steps),
        eta_min=LEARNING_RATE * 0.05,
    )"}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Widen and deepen the micro-ResNet, make the channel multiplier active, and align AdamW plus cosine decay to the actual step budget for better optimization under a fixed-step checker.","secondary_modes":["indexing","summaries","caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_function","function":"build_scheduler","old":"","new":"","anchor":"","text":"","source":"def build_scheduler(optimizer: optim.Optimizer, steps_per_epoch: int):
    if not USE_LR_SCHEDULE:
        return None
    total_steps = TRAIN_MAX_STEPS if TRAIN_MAX_STEPS is not None else steps_per_epoch * 100
    return optim.lr_scheduler.OneCycleLR(
        optimizer,
        max_lr=LEARNING_RATE,
        total_steps=max(1, total_steps),
        pct_start=0.2,
        div_factor=10.0,
        final_div_factor=100.0,
        anneal_strategy="cos",
        cycle_momentum=False,
    )"}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"Swap cosine decay for a one-cycle schedule so the fixed 256-step budget spends more updates near useful learning rates and gets a short warmup instead of decaying almost immediately.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

# --- LR schedule hyperparameters --------------------------------------------

# --- LR schedule hyperparameters --------------------------------------------
USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True

LR_DECAY_FACTOR = 0.1
LR_DECAY_EPOCHS = [60, 80]","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"        channels = BASE_CHANNELS * CHANNEL_MULT
        bias = not USE_BATCHNORM
        stem_layers: list[nn.Module] = [nn.Conv2d(INPUT_CHANNELS, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias)]
        if USE_BATCHNORM:
            stem_layers.append(nn.BatchNorm2d(channels))
        stem_layers.append(nn.ReLU(inplace=True))
        self.stem = nn.Sequential(*stem_layers)
        block_layers: list[nn.Module] = [
            nn.Conv2d(channels, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
        ]
        block_layers.extend(
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM)
            for _ in range(DEPTH)
        )
        self.blocks = nn.Sequential(*block_layers)
        self.pool = nn.AdaptiveAvgPool2d((1, 1))
        self.classifier = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Flatten(),
            nn.Linear(channels, FC_HIDDEN),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            nn.Dropout(DROPOUT_RATE),
            nn.Linear(FC_HIDDEN, NUM_CLASSES),
        )","new":"        channels = BASE_CHANNELS * CHANNEL_MULT
        stage2_channels = channels * 2
        bias = not USE_BATCHNORM
        stem_layers: list[nn.Module] = [nn.Conv2d(INPUT_CHANNELS, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias)]
        if USE_BATCHNORM:
            stem_layers.append(nn.BatchNorm2d(channels))
        stem_layers.append(nn.ReLU(inplace=True))
        self.stem = nn.Sequential(*stem_layers)

        pre_blocks = max(1, DEPTH // 2)
        post_blocks = max(1, DEPTH - pre_blocks)
        block_layers: list[nn.Module] = [
            *[ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM) for _ in range(pre_blocks)],
            nn.Conv2d(channels, stage2_channels, 3, stride=2, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(stage2_channels) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            *[ResidualBlock(stage2_channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM) for _ in range(post_blocks)],
        ]
        self.blocks = nn.Sequential(*block_layers)
        self.pool = nn.AdaptiveAvgPool2d((1, 1))
        self.classifier = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Flatten(),
            nn.Linear(stage2_channels, FC_HIDDEN),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            nn.Dropout(DROPOUT_RATE),
            nn.Linear(FC_HIDDEN, NUM_CLASSES),
        )","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_function","function":"build_scheduler","old":"","new":"","anchor":"","text":"","source":"def build_scheduler(optimizer: optim.Optimizer, steps_per_epoch: int):
    if not USE_LR_SCHEDULE:
        return None
    total_steps = TRAIN_MAX_STEPS if TRAIN_MAX_STEPS is not None else steps_per_epoch * 100
    return optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(
        optimizer,
        T_max=max(1, total_steps),
        eta_min=LEARNING_RATE * 0.05,
    )"}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The prior schedule and regularization ablations regressed, so this re-centers on the better cosine setup while upgrading the network into a small two-stage residual CNN with downsampling, which should give more useful representation power under the same step budget.","secondary_modes":["summaries","caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"DEPTH = 4
BASE_CHANNELS = 12

FC_HIDDEN = 128","new":"DEPTH = 6
BASE_CHANNELS = 12
CHANNEL_MULT = 2
USE_BATCHNORM = True
DROPOUT_RATE = 0.1
FC_HIDDEN = 160","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"        channels = BASE_CHANNELS * CHANNEL_MULT
        stage2_channels = channels * 2
        bias = not USE_BATCHNORM
        stem_layers: list[nn.Module] = [nn.Conv2d(INPUT_CHANNELS, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias)]
        if USE_BATCHNORM:
            stem_layers.append(nn.BatchNorm2d(channels))
        stem_layers.append(nn.ReLU(inplace=True))
        self.stem = nn.Sequential(*stem_layers)

        pre_blocks = max(1, DEPTH // 2)
        post_blocks = max(1, DEPTH - pre_blocks)
        block_layers: list[nn.Module] = [
            *[ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM) for _ in range(pre_blocks)],
            nn.Conv2d(channels, stage2_channels, 3, stride=2, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(stage2_channels) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            *[ResidualBlock(stage2_channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM) for _ in range(post_blocks)],
        ]
        self.blocks = nn.Sequential(*block_layers)
        self.pool = nn.AdaptiveAvgPool2d((1, 1))
        self.classifier = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Flatten(),
            nn.Linear(stage2_channels, FC_HIDDEN),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            nn.Dropout(DROPOUT_RATE),
            nn.Linear(FC_HIDDEN, NUM_CLASSES),
        )","new":"        channels = BASE_CHANNELS * CHANNEL_MULT
        mid_channels = channels * 2
        stage3_channels = channels * 4
        bias = not USE_BATCHNORM
        stem_layers: list[nn.Module] = [nn.Conv2d(INPUT_CHANNELS, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias)]
        if USE_BATCHNORM:
            stem_layers.append(nn.BatchNorm2d(channels))
        stem_layers.append(nn.ReLU(inplace=True))
        self.stem = nn.Sequential(*stem_layers)

        pre_blocks = max(1, DEPTH // 3)
        mid_blocks = max(1, (DEPTH - pre_blocks) // 2)
        post_blocks = max(1, DEPTH - pre_blocks - mid_blocks)
        block_layers: list[nn.Module] = [
            *[ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM) for _ in range(pre_blocks)],
            nn.Conv2d(channels, mid_channels, 3, stride=2, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(mid_channels) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            *[ResidualBlock(mid_channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM) for _ in range(mid_blocks)],
            nn.Conv2d(mid_channels, stage3_channels, 3, stride=2, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(stage3_channels) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            *[ResidualBlock(stage3_channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM) for _ in range(post_blocks)],
        ]
        self.blocks = nn.Sequential(*block_layers)
        self.pool = nn.AdaptiveAvgPool2d((1, 1))
        self.classifier = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Flatten(),
            nn.Linear(stage3_channels, FC_HIDDEN),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            nn.Dropout(DROPOUT_RATE),
            nn.Linear(FC_HIDDEN, NUM_CLASSES),
        )","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Build on the better two-stage residual variant by adding a second downsampling stage and a bit more residual depth, which should improve representation capacity without blowing up compute.","secondary_modes":["summaries","caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"DEPTH = 6
BASE_CHANNELS = 12

USE_BATCHNORM = True

FC_HIDDEN = 192","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The current stage-wise ResNet is still fairly narrow; widening the stem and classifier is the lowest-risk way to add capacity without changing the training dynamics that have worked so far.","secondary_modes":[]}

FC_HIDDEN = 192","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.05)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The 8-block widening regressed, so this reverts to the proven 6-block backbone and adds mild label smoothing to improve generalization without increasing compute.","secondary_modes":["layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.05)","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"        eta_min=LEARNING_RATE * 0.05,","new":"        eta_min=LEARNING_RATE * 0.1,","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"Label smoothing regressed, so revert to the baseline loss and keep the cosine tail less aggressive so the fixed-step run spends more of its budget learning at useful rates.","secondary_modes":["caching","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 3e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"        eta_min=LEARNING_RATE * 0.1,","new":"        eta_min=LEARNING_RATE * 0.05,","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The previous floor increase was a small regression; this restores the better cosine tail and loosens weight decay slightly to reduce residual underfitting on the proven 6-block backbone.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 18","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 192","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 224","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 3e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The 6-block stage-wise model seems width-limited rather than depth-limited, so I am widening the backbone a bit more while restoring the better regularization strength that worked in the best run.","secondary_modes":["caching","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"DEPTH = 6

FC_HIDDEN = 192","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"The wider variant regressed, so this restores the proven 16-channel backbone and makes AdamW slightly more responsive for the short 256-step run by lowering beta2.","secondary_modes":["layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.95)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"The best backbone is restored and beta2=0.99 improved convergence; lowering beta2 further should make AdamW more responsive over the short 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.2e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The faster-adapting AdamW setup still looks slightly conservative on a 256-step budget, so I am nudging the base learning rate up while keeping the current backbone and regularization fixed.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.2e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.95)","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.93)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"The higher learning rate regressed, so this returns to the working step size and makes AdamW slightly more responsive than the current best beta2 setting.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.93)","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.9)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"The beta2 sweep has been moving the right way as it decreases, so I am pushing it a bit further to make AdamW more reactive within the fixed 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.9)","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.92)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"The beta2 sweep appears to peak near 0.93, and 0.9 was too aggressive; this nudges beta2 slightly back up to see whether a short-run sweet spot sits just below the current best.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.92)","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.94)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"The beta2 sweep is tight around the current best, and 0.94 is the nearest unexplored point on the high side of the apparent sweet spot near 0.93.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 9.5e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.94)","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.93)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The best beta2 remains 0.93, and the slightly aggressive 1e-3 step size may still be leaving a bit on the table, so I am nudging the LR down for a cleaner finish.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 9.5e-4","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.93)","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.85, 0.93)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"The learning rate should return to the proven value, and with beta2 already near its sweet spot the remaining unexplored lever is a slightly less inertial beta1 for the short fixed-step run.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}
